channel.title: neodymium isotopes on JD the ocean geochemist
channel.description: Recent content in neodymium isotopes on JD the ocean geochemist

item.title: Deglacial North Pacific circulation
item.description: Summary The circulation of the Pacific Ocean since the Last Glacial Maximum (LGM) remains poorly known, because of the lack of high resolution marine sediment records and carbonate materials that most circulation proxies depend on. We solve these problems in this project by generating authigenic neodymium isotope records, which are not dependent on carbonate archives, from two of the highest resolution (~250 yr) marine cores from the Gulf of Alaska (Figure 1).

item.title: Sedimentary sources of the Rare Earth Elements and Neodymium isotopes
item.description: Investigate how sedimentary sources affect the budgets of Rare Earth Element and Neodymium isotopes in the ocean.

item.title: Neodymium isotope composition of authigenic phases in the Gulf of Alaska
item.description: Develope methods to extract neodymium from authigenic Fe-Mn oxyhydroxides from sediments in the Gulf of Alaska, and investigate how the authigenic phases aquire their unique neodymium isotope signatures.

item.title: Evolution of the Global Overturning Circulation since the Last Glacial Maximum based on marine authigenic neodymium isotopes
Flushing of the deep Pacific Ocean and the deglacial rise of atmospheric CO2 concentrations
The Impact of Benthic Processes on Rare Earth Element and Neodymium Isotope Distributions in the Oceans
